content.text: AutoGen's group chat pattern lets three or more agents share a single conversation thread. A dedicated
agent coordinates who speaks next after each turn. All agents see the same message history, so each participant can read and build on what every other agent said. A

attrs.alt: AutoGen GroupChat: GroupChatManager selects next speaker from agents list; all agents share the GroupChat message history; UserProxy initiates the session

content.text: with a list of agents and a round limit, then wrap it in a
. The manager agent uses its LLM to decide who speaks next unless you override the selection policy.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

# Define specialist agents
researcher = AssistantAgent(
    "researcher",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You research topics and provide factual summaries. Be concise.",
)
coder = AssistantAgent(
    "coder",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You write Python code to solve tasks. Only write code when asked.",
)
critic = AssistantAgent(
    "critic",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You review work for accuracy and completeness. Point out any issues.",
)
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "group_ws", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

# Assemble the group chat
group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
    messages=[],
    max_round=12,
)
manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=group_chat,
    llm_config=llm_config,
)

# Start the conversation
user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    manager,
    message="Research bubble sort, then write a Python implementation, then review it.",
)
controls how the next speaker is chosen:
GroupChatManager uses its LLM to read the conversation and pick the best next speaker
Agents speak in the order they appear in the agents list, cycling
A random agent from the list is picked each turn

# Round-robin: researcher → coder → critic → researcher → ...
group_chat_rr = GroupChat(
    agents=[researcher, coder, critic, user_proxy],
    messages=[],
    max_round=9,
    speaker_selection_method="round_robin",
)

# Custom selection: always route to critic after coder
def custom_selector(last_speaker, groupchat):
    if last_speaker.name == "coder":
        return next(a for a in groupchat.agents if a.name == "critic")
    # Default: let the manager pick
    return None  # None means fall back to auto

group_chat_custom = GroupChat(
    agents=[researcher, coder, critic, user_proxy],
    messages=[],
    max_round=12,
    speaker_selection_method=custom_selector,
)
Fine-tune the speaker graph by specifying which agent-to-agent transitions are allowed or blocked. Pass a dict mapping each agent to the list of agents that can follow it, and set

from autogen import GroupChat

# researcher → coder only; coder → critic only; critic → user_proxy or researcher
allowed_transitions = {
    researcher: [coder],
    coder: [critic],
    critic: [user_proxy, researcher],
    user_proxy: [researcher],
}

group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
    messages=[],
    max_round=12,
    speaker_selection_method="auto",
    allowed_or_disallowed_speaker_transitions=allowed_transitions,
    speaker_transitions_type="allowed",
)
means only the listed next speakers may follow each agent.
means the listed agents are excluded and any other agent may speak. Use
is set, the manager sends a prompt to the LLM asking which agent should speak next. Customise this prompt with
to give the LLM richer instructions about the roles and when each agent should be chosen.

custom_selection_prompt = (
    "You are moderating a group discussion. "
    "The agents are: {roles}. "
    "Given the conversation so far, who should speak next? "
    "Choose 'researcher' for gathering information, "
    "'coder' for writing or debugging code, "
    "'critic' for reviewing accuracy, "
    "'user_proxy' only if the task is fully complete. "
    "Reply with just the agent name."
)

group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
    messages=[],
    max_round=12,
    select_speaker_message_template=custom_selection_prompt,
)
After the conversation ends, inspect the full transcript via
— a list of message dicts with

user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message="Write a function that checks prime numbers.")

# Print the full group chat transcript
for msg in group_chat.messages:
    name = msg.get("name", msg.get("role", "?"))
    content = (msg.get("content") or "")[:100]
    print(f"[{name:15s}] {content}")

# Find all code blocks produced by the coder agent
coder_msgs = [m for m in group_chat.messages if m.get("name") == "coder"]
print(f"\nCoder produced {len(coder_msgs)} messages")

in the group executes code. When the coder writes a Python block, the user proxy runs it and posts the output back into the group chat as a message — other agents (researcher, critic) then see the execution result and can respond to it.

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=8,
    code_execution_config={
        "work_dir": "group_code",
        "use_docker": False,
        "timeout": 60,
    },
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

# user_proxy must be in the agents list so it can execute code blocks
group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
    messages=[],
    max_round=15,
)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=group_chat, llm_config=llm_config)

user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    manager,
    message=(
        "Research the time complexity of quicksort, "
        "implement it in Python with a random pivot, "
        "test it on [3,1,4,1,5,9,2,6], then verify the result is correct."
    ),
)
is reached, (2) any agent sends a message matching
on the user proxy, or (3) all agents decline to speak. Setting a sensible
is important — an LLM-based speaker selector can loop indefinitely without it.

# Standard termination setup for group chats
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config=False,
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
    # max_consecutive_auto_reply is a secondary guard in group chats
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=10,
)

group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
    messages=[],
    max_round=20,    # hard cap — group chat always ends by round 20
)
Test group chat logic by registering deterministic mock reply functions on each agent and asserting the sequence of speakers in the message log.

import pytest
from autogen import (AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent,
                     GroupChat, GroupChatManager)

def mock_agent(name: str, reply: str) -> AssistantAgent:
    a = AssistantAgent(name, llm_config=False, human_input_mode="NEVER")
    a.register_reply(trigger=None,
                     reply_func=lambda r, msgs, s, cfg: (True, reply),
                     position=0)
    return a

def test_round_robin_order():
    a = mock_agent("a", "response A")
    b = mock_agent("b", "response B TERMINATE")
    proxy = UserProxyAgent("proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER",
                           code_execution_config=False,
                           is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))
    gc = GroupChat(agents=[proxy, a, b], messages=[],
                   max_round=4, speaker_selection_method="round_robin")
    mgr = GroupChatManager(groupchat=gc, llm_config=False)
    proxy.initiate_chat(mgr, message="start", max_turns=4)
    speakers = [m.get("name") for m in gc.messages if m.get("name")]
    assert "a" in speakers
    assert "b" in speakers
To continue a group chat after it has ended — for example, to ask a follow-up question without losing the shared context — reinitialise the
with the existing message history from the previous session and set

# First conversation
group_chat = GroupChat(agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
                       messages=[], max_round=12)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=group_chat, llm_config=llm_config)
user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message="Research quicksort.")

# Save the history
saved_messages = list(group_chat.messages)

# Resume — create a new GroupChat seeded with the old messages
group_chat_2 = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder, critic],
    messages=saved_messages,   # restore prior context
    max_round=8,
)
manager_2 = GroupChatManager(groupchat=group_chat_2, llm_config=llm_config)
user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    manager_2,
    message="Now implement quicksort in Python based on what was researched.",
    clear_history=False,   # don't wipe agent histories
)
Group Chat in a FastAPI Endpoint
Expose a group chat as an HTTP endpoint by using the async variants. Create fresh agent and GroupChat instances per request to avoid state contamination between concurrent users.

from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

api = FastAPI()
llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

class GroupTaskRequest(BaseModel):
    task: str

@api.post("/group-task")
async def run_group_task(req: GroupTaskRequest) -> dict:
    # Create fresh instances per request
    researcher = AssistantAgent("researcher", llm_config=llm_config,
        system_message="Research and summarise.")
    coder = AssistantAgent("coder", llm_config=llm_config,
        system_message="Write Python code.")
    proxy = UserProxyAgent(
        "proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER",
        code_execution_config={"work_dir": "/tmp/tasks", "use_docker": False},
        is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
    )
    gc = GroupChat(agents=[proxy, researcher, coder], messages=[], max_round=10)
    mgr = GroupChatManager(groupchat=gc, llm_config=llm_config)

    result = await proxy.a_initiate_chat(mgr, message=req.task)
    return {"summary": result.summary, "turns": len(gc.messages)}
GroupChat holds a shared message list and a list of participating agents; GroupChatManager orchestrates who speaks next.
speaker_selection_method='auto' uses the manager's LLM to select the next speaker; 'round_robin' and 'random' are cheaper alternatives.
allowed_or_disallowed_speaker_transitions constrains which agent-to-agent handoffs are permitted — useful for pipeline enforcement.
Only the UserProxyAgent executes code blocks; the result is posted back into the group chat for all agents to see.
Group chats terminate at max_round, on TERMINATE in any message, or when all agents decline to reply.
Access the full transcript via group_chat.messages — a list of {role, content, name} dicts.
Test group chats by registering mock reply functions and asserting speaker order in the message log.
